info.description: Image upload, compression, transformation, and storage management API.
info.title: Image Pipeline API

post.description: Generates a reset token (15 min expiry). Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration. In production the token would be emailed.
Authenticate with email and password, returns a JWT token valid for 24 hours.
Create a new account with email, name, and password. Password must meet policy: min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 digit, 1 special character.
Set a new password using the reset token from forgot-password. New password must meet password policy.

get.description: Poll the progress of a batch transform or revert operation.

delete.description: Delete a single image by ID. Only the owning user can delete.

get.description: Get a paginated list of the authenticated user's images. Supports search and status filtering.

delete.description: Delete multiple images by IDs. Max 50 per request.

post.description: Revert transformations on multiple images. Batches ≤10 processed synchronously; larger batches enqueued to SQS.
Apply the same transformations to multiple images. Batches ≤10 are processed synchronously; larger batches are enqueued to SQS.
Confirm that files have been uploaded to S3 and enqueue them for compression. Requires an idempotency key.

400.description: Missing idem key or no files

post.description: Validate files and generate presigned S3 URLs. Max 30 files, 100MB each, 500MB total. Allowed types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp.
Cancel a transform that is still in "processing" status.
Remove all applied transformations and restore the compressed version.
post.summary: Revert transform on a single image

post.description: Enqueue transformations (grayscale, sepia, blur, sharpen, invert, resize, crop, watermark, format, remove-bg) for a single image.

get.description: Fetch a single image by its request ID (UUID assigned during prepare).
Returns the user's current storage usage, quota limit, and usage percentage.

message.example: if the email exists, a reset link has been sent
